task: A friend is writing about how people seem to change over the years and wants a plain, matter-of-fact line to use in a note. I’m trying to help them phrase it without making it sound dramatic or overly philosophical.

turns.agent_response:
Here are a few plain, matter-of-fact options:

- People change over time.
- People do change as the years go by.
- Over the years, people change.
- People are not always the same from one year to the next.
- Time changes people.

If you want, I can make it sound more:
- neutral
- a little warmer
- more direct
- more like a note or letter

user_simulator.text: Too plain. I wanted something with a little more weight but still not flowery. "People change over time" is fine, but it sounds like something a schoolkid would write. Give me a few that sound more natural in an actual note.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are a few that feel more natural in a note, with a little more weight but still plain:

- People change as the years go by.
- No one stays quite the same over time.
- Time changes people in ways you can’t always see right away.
- People don’t stay exactly the same from one year to the next.
- A lot can change in a person over the years.

If you want, I can make them sound:
- a little more personal
- a little more formal
- more reflective, but still plain

user_simulator.text:
Better, but still a little too tidy.

I’d want something with a bit more bite, like:
- People don’t stay the same for long.
- Over the years, people change in ways you notice later.
- A person can change a lot as time goes on.
- You don’t stay quite the same after enough years.
- Time has a way of changing people.

That’s closer. If you want, make them sound a little more like an actual handwritten note — less polished, more natural.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are a few that sound a little less polished and more like something someone might actually write in a note:

- People don’t stay the same for long.
- Over the years, people change in ways you notice later.
- A person can change a lot as time goes on.
- You don’t stay quite the same after enough years.
- Time has a way of changing people.

A few slightly rougher ones, if they want it to feel even more natural:

- People change, whether they mean to or not.
- After a while, people aren’t quite who they were.
- You can see it later — people do change.
- Time changes people more than they admit.
- Nobody stays exactly the same.

If you want, I can make them sound:
- a little more warm
- a little more blunt
- more like a personal note

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. But a couple still sound too neat.

I’d want the rougher ones, but not the ones that sound like a quote on a calendar. More like something a person would jot down and move on.

Give me 5-6 that feel like:
- plain handwritten note
- a little worn-in
- not trying to sound smart
- not too sentimental

Something like that.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are 6 that stay plain and a little rough around the edges:

- After a while, people aren’t quite who they were.
- Time changes people more than they admit.
- Nobody stays exactly the same.

- You don’t stay the same for long.

If you want, I can make them even more:

- worn-in
- personal
- or a little colder

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a couple of those sound a little too neat, like they got edited. I’d want the rougher edge, something a person would actually scribble and not think twice about.

Give me 5-6 more in that same lane, but a little less polished still.
